creative_brief: Clean, personal layout with Rachel's photo. Emphasize the journey from struggle to hope with warm, authentic typography.
subject: I thought I'd lost everything
preheader: Then I discovered families who actually cared where their food came from
body:
Three years ago, I was ready to give up farming.

Drought had decimated our pastures. Our cattle were stressed. And the wholesalers kept squeezing our margins until we were barely breaking even on beef that took us two years to raise with love.

I remember sitting in our kitchen, staring at another rejection letter, thinking: "There has to be families out there who care about how their food is raised. But how do I reach them?"

That's when I met the FarmThru team.

They said something that changed everything: "What if we could connect you directly to Sydney families? No wholesalers. No middlemen taking their cut. Just your regenerative beef going straight from your farm to their table."

Today, our grass-fed beef goes direct to families via FarmThru's Brookvale hub. The cattle graze on healthy soil. We earn a fair price. And families know exactly where their food comes from — because we'll show them.

It's not just our farm anymore. Little Yarran, Bundarra Farm, Collins Wild Caught — we're all part of something bigger. A grocery store that actually gives a damn about farmers and the families we feed.

This is why I'm backing FarmThru's crowdfunding campaign. Because every family deserves to know their farmer's name.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.
